html:
Exclusive to:  

Guacamole is a Meat Food item introduced in the Reign of Giants DLC. It is cooked in the Crock Pot by combining 1 Moleworm and 1 Raw Cactus Flesh.

Adding Corn and Fish may yield Fish Tacos instead. Adding 2 Raw Drumsticks may result in Turkey Dinner. Adding 1.0 unit of Meats and 1 Vegetables may result in Spicy Chili. Adding Mandrake may produce Mandrake Soup.

Many combinations to make this recipe would result in a net loss to hunger compared to eating the individual components. It is suggested to use a filler such as Twigs to improve its Hunger restoration.

Wurt cannot eat this, despite calling it yummy in her inspection quote.

Prerequisites

  • Requires: 
  • Requires: 
  • Excludes: 

Recipe

×2

Examples

×2

 A recipe for Guacamole that extenuates hunger value

×2

50% chance to result in Fish Tacos

×2

50% chance to result in Turkey Dinner
50% chance to result in Spicy Chili

Tips

  • Placing Moleworms close to Cactus will allow for easy mass production of Guacamole.

Trivia

  • Requiring an actual Moleworm as an ingredient for this recipe is a pun on the words "Mole" and "Guacamole".
  • Cactus Flesh is a popular food item in the same region that Guacamole comes from, though it is not traditionally a part of guacamole.
    • Guacamole originates from Mexico.
  • The literal translation of the word "Guacamole" into English is "Avocado Sauce". Guaca = Avocado, Mole = Sauce.
  • Wilson's quote is a possible pun with Amedeo Avogadro (An Italian physicist known for his contribution for molecular physics) with the word "avocado" and the fact of "mole" being a scientific measuring unit.
  • Wanda's quote is a possible reference to "The Guacamole Song" by Dr. Jean.

Gallery

  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

Adding Corn and Fish may yield Fish Tacos instead. Adding 2 Raw Drumsticks may result in Turkey Dinner. Adding 1.0 unit of Meats and 1 Vegetables may result in Spicy Chili. Adding Mandrake may produce Mandrake Soup.
Many combinations to make this recipe would result in a net loss to hunger compared to eating the individual components. It is suggested to use a filler such as Twigs to improve its Hunger restoration.

'''Guacamole''' is a [[Meats|Meat]] [[Food]] [[item]] introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC. It is cooked in the [[Crock Pot]] by combining 1 [[Moleworm]] and 1 [[Cactus Flesh|Raw Cactus Flesh]].

Adding [[Corn]] and [[Fishes|Fish]] may yield [[Fish Tacos]] instead. Adding 2 [[Drumsticks|Raw Drumsticks]] may result in [[Turkey Dinner]]. Adding 1.0 unit of [[Meats]] and 1 [[Vegetables]] may result in [[Spicy Chili]]. Adding [[Mandrake]] may produce [[Mandrake Soup]].

Many combinations to make this recipe would result in a net loss to hunger compared to eating the individual components. It is suggested to use a filler such as [[Twigs]] to improve its [[Hunger]] restoration.

[[Wurt]] cannot eat this, despite calling it yummy in her inspection quote.
== Prerequisites ==
* Requires: [[File:Moleworm.png|link=Moleworm]]
* Requires: [[File:Cactus Flesh.png|link=Cactus Flesh]]
* Excludes: [[File:Fruits Crock Pot.png|link=Fruits]]

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Moleworm
|item2 = Cactus Flesh
|item3 = Filler
|count3 = 2
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Guacamole
}}

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Placing Moleworms close to Cactus will allow for easy mass production of Guacamole.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Requiring an actual Moleworm as an ingredient for this recipe is a pun on the words "Mole" and "Guacamole".
* Cactus Flesh is a popular food item in the same region that Guacamole comes from, though it is not traditionally a part of guacamole.
** Guacamole originates from [[Wikipedia:Mexico|Mexico]].
* The literal translation of the word "Guacamole" into English is "Avocado Sauce". Guaca = Avocado, Mole = Sauce.
* Wilson's quote is a possible pun with [[Wikipedia:Amedeo Avogadro|Amedeo Avogadro]] (An Italian physicist known for his contribution for molecular physics) with the word "avocado" and the fact of "mole" being a scientific measuring unit.
*Wanda's quote is a possible reference to "The Guacamole Song" by Dr. Jean.
